title: rosa gallica var. pannonica
intro: Rosa gallica var. pannonica, also known as the Hungarian rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a variant of the Gallic rose, known for its fragrant pink flowers and medicinal properties. Rosa gallica var. pannonica has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin problems, wounds, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
